accept: From a fresh GOMODCACHE with no GOPRIVATE / no git auth, 'go install github.com/aac/act/cmd/act@latest' completes successfully.
The installed binary's 'act help' output matches the head-of-main help output (i.e. the published tag is at or near HEAD, not 192 commits stale).
Verified in a clean /tmp directory by running the installed binary through 'act init' + 'act create' + 'act ready' end-to-end.
payload.description: act-6051 picked 'go install github.com/aac/act/cmd/act@latest' as the canonical bootstrap and the README + 'act help' both document it. End-to-end verification from a fresh GOMODCACHE (no auth, no local cache) fails today: the repo is private (gh repo view aac/act --visibility = PRIVATE) and sum.golang.org returns 404 for the module, so 'go install …@latest' cannot resolve for a friend's agent. With GOPRIVATE=github.com/aac and Andrew's git auth it succeeds but pulls the cached v0.1.0 tag, which is 192 commits behind HEAD as of c8ae75f. To make the canonical pitch in README.md and 'act help' actually work for a fresh agent in another repo: (1) flip aac/act public on GitHub (history is clean; no secrets in op-log JSON or docs), then (2) cut a fresh release tag at or near HEAD so '@latest' resolves to current code rather than the stale 192-commit-old v0.1.0. The current v0.1.0 GitHub Release is still in Draft state (act-e6a5 covers the tap/curl side separately).
payload.title: publish aac/act so 'go install …@latest' resolves to current code from a fresh GOPATH
